methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $43M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Morpheus Space is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Daniel Bock and István Lőrincz that has raised $43M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public Space & Aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

The primary applicable method is stage-progression analysis. Using the $43M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, the method implies a post-progression valuation of $86M. This reflects the expected equity value appreciation between current stage and the next financing round. Peer-median triangulation was not feasible due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Space & Aerospace cohort—only 2 adjacent-stage peers were identified against a threshold of 3+. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples could not be applied; while the sector median stands at 25.8x across the 6 public companies, Morpheus Space's revenue is not disclosed, precluding reliable multiple application.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $73M (low) to $99M (high), centered on $86M. This range is derived from stage-progression methodology alone. Key uncertainties include the company's undisclosed revenue run-rate, which would strengthen public-comp anchoring, and the limited availability of late-stage private comparable transactions in the aerospace subsector.
